_rules.format_v1: blob file = version byte 0x01 || nonce(12) || AES-256-GCM ct||tag; key = per-blob DEK (32 bytes); AAD = the 1-byte header (ADR-0021)
_rules.address: SHA-256 over the ENTIRE blob file, lowercase hex (RFC-0002 §6 'hash of ciphertext' as pinned by ADR-0021)
_rules.wrap: wrapped key = nonce(12) || AES-256-GCM ct(32)||tag(16), hex-encoded; run-key wrap: KEK = master key, AAD = raw run id bytes; DEK wrap: KEK = run key, AAD = the 64-char lowercase-hex blob address (ASCII bytes)
_rules.provenance: expected values computed by an independent implementation (Python cryptography AESGCM) from these rules, never by the code under test; all key material is synthetic patterned bytes

cases.name: blob-v1-empty
cases.note: ADR-0021 format v1: empty plaintext is legal; minimum file is 29 bytes

cases.name: blob-v1-text
cases.note: ADR-0021 format v1: utf-8 text body ('hello, vault'); RFC-0002 §6 address is over ciphertext, never plaintext

cases.name: blob-v1-binary
cases.note: ADR-0021 format v1: arbitrary binary body including 0x00 and high bytes

cases.name: wrap-run-key
cases.note: ADR-0021 / RFC-0002 D9: run key wrapped under the master key, AAD = raw run id bytes

cases.name: wrap-dek
cases.note: ADR-0021 / RFC-0002 D9: per-blob DEK wrapped under the run key, AAD = the blob-v1-text address (64-char hex, ASCII bytes)
